Honestly, it's been tough. I've been getting really anxious and irritable, and the cravings are just... ugh. I feel like I'm walking on eggshells all the time, waiting for the other shoe to drop. And when I'm working, it's even worse. I'm just worried that I'll slip up and have a cigarette, and then I'll be right back where I started.

Well, I think if I could just find ways to manage my anxiety and cravings better, that would be a huge help. And maybe if I could find some support, like a buddy or something, that would be great. But it's hard when all my friends are smokers, you know? It feels like I'm the only one trying to quit.

Honestly, it's been a struggle. I quit about a month ago, but I'm still dealing with anxiety and irritability. I feel like I'm on edge all the time. And the cravings are intense, especially in the morning and after meals. I'm just not sure how much more of this I can take.

I think it's just that I genuinely enjoyed smoking. I miss the social aspect of it, you know? My friends and I would smoke during breaks at work, and it was just a part of who I was. Now I feel like I've lost that part of myself. And on top of that, I'm worried about gaining weight. I've always been self-conscious about my body image, and the thought of gaining weight is making me anxious.

I guess it was just a way to take a break, you know? A way to relax and unwind. And it was something I shared with my friends. Now I feel like I'm stuck on the outside looking in, watching them smoke and feeling left out. It's hard to explain.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And it's hard because I know I need to stay quit, but it's hard to resist the temptation when I'm around others who are smoking.

Honestly, I think just having someone to talk to would help. And maybe some tips on how to manage the anxiety and cravings. I feel like I'm just trying to white-knuckle it right now, and it's not working.

motivation_reason: don't want to go through quitting again

session_notes.note: Honestly, I'm not really sure why I'm here. I mean, I know smoking isn't great for me, but I've been doing it for 20 years and I'm still healthy as an ox. I'm not really sure I buy into all the hype about how bad it is for you.

Well, like I said, I'm healthy and active. I exercise regularly, I eat well... I just don't think I'm the typical smoker profile. And to be honest, I've tried quitting before and it was a disaster. I don't want to go through that again.

I think it's a bit of both, to be honest. I do enjoy the break that smoking gives me during my work sessions. It's a way for me to clear my head and recharge. And, I guess, it's just something I've always done. But, I don't know... maybe I'm just making excuses.

Hi, thanks for seeing me. Yeah, I've been trying to quit for a while now. I've been smoking for about 20 years, and I'm just so tired of feeling like I'm controlled by cigarettes. I've tried a few times before, but I always end up going back to them. This time, I'm really determined to quit for good.

Honestly, it's just getting too expensive. Between the cost of cigarettes and the doctor's bills for my asthma, it's just not worth it anymore. Plus, my kids are getting older, and I don't want them to see me smoking anymore. I want to be a good role model for them.

Well, I've been trying to cut down on my own, but it's not working very well. I've also been looking into those prescription medications, but they're just so expensive. I don't know how I'm going to afford them.
